title: [InstCombine] Fold intrinsics over multi-use selects when the intrinsic is the only user
summary:
The developers generalized the pattern by recognizing that the core optimization—folding an operation into a select when the select's operands are constants—isn't specific to `sincos`. They extended it to any intrinsic with constant-foldable arguments, like `fshl`/`fshr` (rotates), by modifying the condition from `hasOneUse()` to `hasOneUser()`. This allows the select to have multiple uses as long as it feeds only one instruction, enabling safe transformation without affecting other uses.

The test suite demonstrates the generalization: it covers both left/right rotates, scalar/vector types, splat/non-splat constants, poison elements, and multi-use scenarios. Each test validates that constant folding works correctly after moving the select inside the intrinsic. For developers, this shows how to identify a generic condition (constant-foldable operands) and relax constraints (use → user) to safely broaden an optimization while adding comprehensive tests for edge cases.
